mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in Industrial Production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output that lifts corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of months when INDPRO increases month-over-month over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated Federal Funds rate above 5% restricts credit availability and raises discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and decreases in retail sales (RSAFS) signal coordinated labor and consumer demand deterioration that amplifies recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases month-over-month and RSAFS decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The interaction term of UNRATE and RSAFS produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month declines in housing starts (HOUST) foreshadow reduced construction activity and mortgage-backed credit contraction, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%, month-over-month declines in HOUST are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates resilient consumer demand that supports top-line revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) above 0.3% intensifies Federal Reserve tightening expectations, raising real discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL accelerates by more than 0.3% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) reveal a synchronized contraction across labor and manufacturing sectors, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month and INDPRO declines month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint decline signal produces positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals aggregate demand expansion that lifts broad-based corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly increases in GDPC1 are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year window.
